name: bfinject
summary: bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.

documentation.readme: bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.
usage_sections: Jailbreak your iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2 device with Electra >= b7
Jailbreak your iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2 device with LiberiOS >= 11.0.3
Launch the target app into which you will inject your shared library

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

examples: bfinject -P Reddit.app -l /path/to/evil.dylib   # Injects evil.dylib into the Reddit app
bfinject -p 1234 -L cycript                     # Inject Cycript into PID
bfinject -p 4566 -l /path/to/evil.dylib         # Injects the .dylib of your choice into PID
Instead of specifying the PID with -p, bfinject can search for the correct PID based on the app name.
Cs-iPhone:~ root# bash bfinject -P Reddit -L test

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the iOS dylib injection category. Fits when the engagement needs: bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `tar xvf bfinject.tar`; `export PATH=$PATH:/jb/usr/bin:/jb/bin:/jb/sbin:/jb/usr/sbin:/jb/usr/local/bin:`. bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need iOS dylib injection, use bfinject because bfinject Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for bfinject.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tar xvf bfinject.tar` as documented upstream for bfinject.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `export PATH=$PATH:/jb/usr/bin:/jb/bin:/jb/sbin:/jb/usr/sbin:/jb/usr/local/bin:` as documented upstream for bfinject.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cd /jb` as documented upstream for bfinject.
When chaining iOS dylib injection tools, pair bfinject with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bfinject --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: bfinject -P Reddit.app -l /path/to/evil.dylib   # Injects evil.dylib into the Reddit app
bfinject -p 1234 -L cycript                     # Inject Cycript into PID
bfinject -p 4566 -l /path/to/evil.dylib         # Injects the .dylib of your choice into PID
Instead of specifying the PID with -p, bfinject can search for the correct PID based on the app name.
Cs-iPhone:~ root# bash bfinject -P Reddit -L test
